Title: Co‐regulation of indole glucosinolates and camalexin biosynthesis by CPK5/CPK6 and MPK3/MPK6 signaling pathways

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CDPK/CPK family serine/threonine protein kinase with EF-hand calcium-binding regulatory domain
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Botrytis cinerea infection; calcium signaling; dexamethasone in inducible CPK5-VK system
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of indole glucosinolates and camalexin; also implicated in secretion-related transporter gene induction
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB51; MYB122; CYP81F2; IGMT1; IGMT2; CYP71A13; PAD3; PDR12; PEN3; WRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates/upregulates expression of indole glucosinolate and camalexin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein kinase signaling leading to transcriptional activation of downstream metabolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CPK6; MPK3; MPK6; MYB51; WRKY33; PDR12; PEN3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator required for full pathogen-induced accumulation of 4MI3G and camalexin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes accumulation of 4-methoxyindole-3-ylmethylglucosinolate and camalexin and enhances resistance to Botrytis cinerea
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antimicrobial defense metabolites contributing to resistance against Botrytis cinerea
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedlings; secreted camalexin in culture medium/apoplastic space
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: induced/activated in defense context; functional evidence from DEX-inducible constitutively active transgene
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: acts in 12-day-old seedlings and 4-week-old leaves during B. cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; dexamethasone induction; NA-PP1 in MPK6SR-related assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: constitutive/overexpression-related activation can cause spontaneous cell death; full-length CPK5 requires calcium for activation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance disease-protective indole glucosinolate/camalexin metabolism, but activation should be conditional/tissue-specific to avoid cell-death penalties
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (cpk5, cpk5 cpk6); inducible gain-of-function CPK5-VK; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling by HPLC and fluorescence assay; genetic crosses with myb51, mkk4 mkk5, and MPK6SR backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Inducible activation of constitutively active CPK5 upregulated genes in indole glucosinolate and camalexin pathways. 2. CPK5 activation strongly increased I3G, 4MI3G, and especially camalexin accumulation. 3. Loss of CPK5 together with CPK6 reduced B. cinerea-induced 4MI3G and camalexin and compromised resistance. 4. MYB51 acts genetically downstream of CPK5 for indole glucosinolate induction. 5. CPK5 acts independently but cooperatively with the MPK3/MPK6 pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The active truncated CPK5-VK construct lacks C-terminal calcium regulatory/autoinhibitory domains; full-length CPK5 transgene alone was insufficient to trigger strong downstream responses without stimulus.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CDPK/CPK family serine/threonine protein kinase with EF-hand calcium-binding regulatory domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of indole glucosinolates and camalexin
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: with CPK5 redundantly supports induction of MYB51 and biosynthetic genes including CYP81F2; IGMT1; IGMT2; CYP71A13; PAD3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive redundant regulator of pathogen-induced metabolic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: redundant positive regulator required together with CPK5 for full induction of 4MI3G and camalexin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports accumulation of 4MI3G and camalexin and resistance to Botrytis cinerea redundantly with CPK5
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedlings; secreted camalexin in medium/apoplastic space
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: defense-associated functional role inferred from mutant response
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: functions in 12-day-old seedlings and 4-week-old leaves during B. cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: single mutant showed little phenotype, indicating redundancy with CPK5
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely useful in combination with CPK5 rather than alone for engineering defense metabolite induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single and double mutant analysis; RT-qPCR of pathway genes; metabolite profiling after B. cinerea inoculation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cpk6 single mutant showed little change in resistance or metabolite induction. 2. Combined loss with CPK5 significantly reduced pathogen-induced 4MI3G and camalexin. 3. CPK6 acts redundantly with CPK5 in defense metabolite regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is largely redundant with CPK5 in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Botrytis cinerea infection; MKK4/MKK5 signaling; DEX-inducible NtMEK2DD system
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of indole glucosinolates and camalexin
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB51; ERF6; WRKY33; CYP81F2; IGMT1; IGMT2; PAD3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive activation of downstream transcription factors and metabolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: required with MPK6 for full pathogen-induced 4MI3G and camalexin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes 4MI3G and camalexin accumulation and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq comparison used prior DD dataset; accession not separately provided here
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; DEX induction of NtMEK2DD; NA-PP1 in MPK6SR system
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mpk3 mpk6 double mutant is lethal, necessitating conditional chemical-genetic rescue system
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway for enhancing defense metabolites, but manipulation requires conditional or partial activation because of essential developmental roles
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: chemical-genetic mpk3 mpk6 rescue system (MPK6SR + NA-PP1); inducible NtMEK2DD activation; metabolite profiling; mutant interaction analyses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPK3/MPK6 pathway positively regulates 4MI3G and camalexin accumulation. 2. Combined loss with CPK5/CPK6 causes stronger reduction than either pathway alone. 3. Activation-induced metabolite accumulation occurs independently of CPK5/CPK6.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper MPK3 function is mainly demonstrated as part of the MPK3/MPK6 module rather than individually.

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Botrytis cinerea infection; MKK4/MKK5 signaling; DEX-inducible NtMEK2DD system
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of indole glucosinolates and camalexin
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB51; ERF6; WRKY33; CYP81F2; IGMT1; IGMT2; PAD3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive activation of downstream transcription factors and metabolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: required with MPK3 for full pathogen-induced 4MI3G and camalexin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes 4MI3G and camalexin accumulation and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq comparison used prior DD dataset; accession not separately provided here
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; DEX induction of NtMEK2DD; NA-PP1 in MPK6SR system
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mpk3 mpk6 double mutant is lethal, necessitating conditional chemical-genetic rescue system
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway for enhancing defense metabolites, but manipulation requires conditional or partial activation because of essential developmental roles
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: chemical-genetic mpk3 mpk6 rescue system (MPK6SR + NA-PP1); inducible NtMEK2DD activation; metabolite profiling; mutant interaction analyses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPK3/MPK6 pathway positively regulates 4MI3G and camalexin accumulation. 2. Combined loss with CPK5/CPK6 causes stronger reduction than either pathway alone. 3. Activation-induced metabolite accumulation occurs independently of CPK5/CPK6.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper MPK6 function is mainly demonstrated as part of the MPK3/MPK6 module rather than individually.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: upstream signaling controlling 4MI3G and camalexin biosynthesis through MPK3/MPK6
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive upstream activator of MPK3/MPK6-dependent metabolic induction
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: with MKK5 in redundant upstream MAPKK module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream positive regulator necessary for strong pathogen-induced 4MI3G and camalexin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes pathogen-induced 4MI3G and camalexin accumulation and resistance through MPK3/MPK6 activation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; DEX induction in CPK5-VK epistasis tests
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mkk4 mkk5 mutant has residual activity because of approximately 10% residual MKK4 activity in TILLING mutant
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents an upstream control node, but broad developmental/defense roles may limit direct deployment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mkk4 mkk5 mutant phenotyping; MPK3/MPK6 phosphorylation assays; metabolite profiling; quadruple mutant analysis with cpk5 cpk6
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MKK4/MKK5 act upstream of MPK3/MPK6 in B. cinerea-induced 4MI3G and camalexin biosynthesis. 2. mkk4 mkk5 mutants show reduced metabolite accumulation and increased susceptibility. 3. Combined loss with cpk5 cpk6 further lowers 4MI3G.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as a redundant pair with MKK5 rather than individually.

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: CPK5/CPK6 signaling; MPK3/MPK6 signaling; Botrytis cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: indole glucosinolate biosynthetic pathway genes; genetically linked to I3G and 4MI3G production
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive regulator of IGS biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: indispensable downstream regulator for CPK5/CPK6-mediated indole glucosinolate induction in shoots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for CPK5-induced I3G and 4MI3G accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly induced after CPK5 activation; induced by B. cinerea in wild type and compromised in cpk5 cpk6
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea infection; DEX induction in CPK5-VK system
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising transcriptional regulator for boosting defensive indole glucosinolates in shoots; should be balanced against possible defense-growth tradeoffs not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; genetic cross of CPK5-VK with myb51 mutant; metabolite profiling of I3G and 4MI3G
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB51 expression is strongly induced by CPK5 activation and reduced in cpk5 cpk6 after pathogen challenge. 2. Loss of MYB51 dramatically suppresses CPK5-induced I3G and 4MI3G accumulation. 3. MYB51 functions downstream of CPK5/CPK6 in B. cinerea-induced indole glucosinolate biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes MYB51 regulates IGS biosynthesis mainly in shoots.
